subject: RE: Weird behavior after 3D denoising of reconstructed tomograms
body:
Hi Linhua,
 
I think this is normal, the network splits the tomogram into small cubes and denoises the cubes independently – because you’ve used so few iterations it hasn’t learnt how to do the reconstruction well yet and that makes the borders between the cubes very visible! Looks like you’ll have nice denoised tomograms after some more training.
 
Best,
 
Alister
 
Sent from 
Mail
 for Windows 10
 
From: 
Linhua Tai
Sent: 
Thursday, April 23, 2020 12:47 PM
To: 
Warp
Subject: 
Weird behavior after 3D denoising of reconstructed tomograms
 
Dear all,
I've been testing Warp's 3D denoising of tomograms, I've got it running successfully, and the command I use is:
noise2map.exe --observation1 even --observation2 odd --dont_flatten_spectrum --iterations 800 --batchsize 1 --gpuid_network 0 --gpuid_preprocess 0.
 
I'm using 800 iterations in order to see whether this program could be running properly, but the output tomogram have a really strange look, as attached, does anybody know why the output looks like this?
 
Thank you very much in advance!
 
Cheers,
Linhua Tai
